ai_overview: Viktor's Stakeholder Digest email was unavailable for 03/05/2026 — Microsoft 365 authentication is pending and this digest is built from Slack and Jira sources only. Sunday 3 May was universally quiet across all 22 tracked team members, consistent with the eve of the UK Early May Bank Holiday (4 May). The week ending 1 May was notably productive: Delivery Cycle 8 closed on 30 April with 136 story points (announced by Chloe Christie in #gdc-pod), and Delivery Cycle 9 was formally kicked off with Josh Smith and Chloe Christie publishing planned features in #gdc-core on 1 May — including Brand Carousel, Ace AI Navigation Improvements, CA/FR Casino Page Oplist Redesign, World Cup Predictor Game, and Group Bets Widget. Ciara Carroll published comprehensive Claude Design group guidelines on 29 April covering etiquette and the gambling.com design system (3,000+ components, Orion GitHub integration, full brand assets). Jessica Dordevic is in the final design stages of the Tinder Widget (GDCU-8546), with matched-state UI finalised on 30 April. Robin John and Shane McGrath cleared all DC8 release tickets on 30 April. Novica Vukobratovic is actively progressing GDCU-8877 (Phone Number Field in Email Confirmation Admin Panel), with the ticket updated early on 4 May indicating development resuming post-weekend. Vic Dadson remains on medical sick leave from 21 April. Mike Pett is off from 30 April until Thursday 7 May. No blockers or hotfixes flagged on 3 May.

people.name: Vic Dadson
people.summary: Remains on doctor-signed medical sick leave from 21 April (signed off for a fortnight). Mike DM'd on 23 April with encouraging news about a data engineering conversation with Chris in BI team — Nemanja and the data team will provide support and knowledge-sharing on her return. No Slack or Jira activity on 3 May.

people.name: Jessica Dordevic
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). Most recent activity 30 April: in DMs with Mike she finalised Tinder Widget (GDCU-8546) matched-state UI decisions — agreeing to hide the interested/pass buttons post-match and remove the word 'betting' from the match label to accommodate casino variants. On 29 April she shared DE HPC article Figma designs in #gdc-product-design-pod. Chloe noted in the sprint close (30 Apr) that Tinder design should be closeable shortly.

people.name: Miguel Migneco
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). On 30 April DM'd Mike to flag he is off next Tuesday (6 May). On 29 April active in #gdc-product-led-growth demonstrating his Claude Design workflow for the Group Bets widget — rapid iteration in Claude before Figma polish, citing a 15-minute Claude prototype that would normally take 3+ hours. Noted designs still require converting for final dev handoff.

people.name: Ciara Carroll
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). On 1 May raised GDCU-8947 (Cosmo Cash Machine) in #gdc-product-design-pod and was asking about Figma file breakpoints for dev-ready files on 28 April. On 29 April published a comprehensive Claude Design usage guidelines post to #core-product and #gdc-pod — covering group etiquette, capabilities, and the gambling.com design system (3,000+ components, full brand assets, Orion GitHub integration, with a live preview URL shared). On 28 April coordinated Claude Design roadmap next steps with Mike and shared CA/FR oplist card redesigns in #gdc-product-led-growth.

people.name: Josh Smith
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). On 1 May published the new Card Carousel shortcode announcement in #gdc-nonen-sync (Confluence docs and test page link included). Also opened a design decision thread in #gdc-product-design-pod on the Carousel Banner Shortcode (GDCU-8787) — proposing to reuse the Simple Banner shortcode with arrows to reduce editor complexity. On 30 April coordinated navigation bar bug investigation with Mike via DM, confirming Robin and Shane cleared the DC8 release QA. Preparing DC9 sprint planning.

people.name: Chloe Christie
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). On 1 May published comprehensive DC8 release summary and DC9 high-priority planned work in #gdc-core — DC9 features include Brand Carousel, Ace Navigation Improvements, CA/FR Casino Page Oplist Redesign, Viktor AI CWV Recommendations, SlotsLaunch Game Grid, Experts Carousel Shortcode, World Cup Predictor Game, and Group Bets Widget. On 30 April announced DC8 closure at 136 SPs in #gdc-pod. On 29 April coordinated World Cup prioritisation with Mike via DM, flagging FTP WC game, Cosmo reskin, and Group Bets as immediate priorities.

people.name: Carlos Blauth
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). Last DM with Mike was 9 April confirming Ace AI feedback report volumes (one production report per day is correct — staging reports previously disabled). No Slack or Jira activity visible in the past three weeks. Involved in FTP App and deep-link betslip project based on channel membership.

people.name: Eoin McCarthy
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). Last DM with Mike was 22 April — confirmed Betting Tips Hub (GDCU-8512) load-more pagination (~30 tips) should be manageable from a page size perspective via the Matrix API. Named as stakeholder on GDCU-8877 (phone number email form ticket currently In Progress with Novica). No Slack channel activity visible in the past two weeks.

people.name: Harden Wallace
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). Last DM with Mike was 24 April — provided a detailed technical assessment on why a single-winner Cosmo Wheel iPhone prize lock is not currently possible (no RNG-scoped winner mechanism exists; manual boost would raise overall win rate without controlling for a single winner). Recommended raising a proper ticket rather than using a workaround. Tagged in #6amigos on 29 April by Gabriel regarding Tailwind CSS grid classes for content layouts.

people.name: Kelvin Yee
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). Last DM with Mike was 9 April — proposed using Site Article Categories with a 'Tips' tag combined with article publish timestamps to filter stale racing tips from the News Hub Most Popular section. No Slack or Jira activity visible since then.

people.name: Milan Nikolic
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). Last DM with Mike was 26 March regarding the Global Strategy Hub (GDCU-8653) — confirmed the v1 approach is to serve the page in English with localised casino results. Previously flagged in #gdc-high-agency (13 March) that 5pm meetings are too late for Serbia. No Slack or Jira activity visible in April 2026.

people.name: Nebojsa Stanic
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). Last DM with Mike was December 2025 — demonstrated a Tinder-style casino card prototype for the AI Day session. No Slack or Jira activity visible in 2026.

people.name: Novica Vukobratovic
people.summary: No Slack activity on 3 May (Sunday). GDCU-8877 (Add Field to Manage Phone Numbers on Email Confirmation Form Pages in Admin Panel) was updated at 07:28 BST on 4 May and is currently In Progress — indicating active development resuming post-weekend. The ticket covers a toggleable phone number field (with mandatory option) for email landing pages (/e and /p) via the admin panel, scoped per URL without impacting other geos or pages. Stakeholders include Chloe Christie and Eoin McCarthy.

people.name: Sanja Budic
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). Last DM with Mike was 7 April (deep-link betslip project kickoff call). On 8 April in #gdc-deep-link-betslip-project she reported the /api/mcp/deep_links endpoint for Bookies brand returning responses above 5 seconds — a performance concern flagged to Alex Kornilov. No Slack channel or Jira activity visible since.

people.name: Robin John
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). On 30 April posted in #gdc-pod confirming QA sign-off for all DC8 release tickets — all clear, with a note that a few content-related issues need separate logging. Josh acknowledged and said he would coordinate with Shane. Last meaningful contribution was the DC8 full release QA clearance.

people.name: Shane McGrath
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). On 30 April raised a bug in #gdc-pod: global pages on mobile are not geo-locating burger menu links (always redirecting to UK geo) — queried whether already raised. Josh confirmed he would investigate. Last DM with Mike was 24 March regarding IUJ duplicate account handling edge case with Google sign-up. Co-signed DC8 QA clearance with Robin.

people.name: Dean Ryan
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). On 30 April active in DMs with Mike discussing logging tipster success rates (confirmed data exists and will be updated) and an odds formatting issue in horse racing tips widgets. Mike shared the World Cup Predictor game Vercel prototype with him at end of day for review.

people.name: Michael Butlin
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). On 30 April active in DMs with Mike until late (mentioned working until 2am) — contributing quiz question copy options for the World Cup Predictor game and reviewing the Vercel prototype. Mike advised rest and flagged he is off until Thursday. Active engagement on WC Predictor feature development.

people.name: Dan Smullen
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). On 28 April active in DMs with Mike navigating Claude Design access — Mike helped him locate the design canvas via the easel icon. Last meaningful channel activity was 15 April in #gdc-f2p-apps flagging F2P game issues (some games not working) and the Cosmo Spins 24-hour wait bug post-login.

people.name: Gabriel Cornoiu
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). On 1 May active in #gdc-nonen-sync (asking about Card Carousel Confluence docs) and #gdc-pod (reporting an Admin Panel issue Dean flagged and checking Global HP offer language display). Also available for morning DMs per #gdc-pdmt post on 1 May. On 29 April in #gdc-pdmt reminded the team about the Learning Hub going live with the weekly release. DM'd with Mike on 29 April re: Greenhouse interview candidate process.

people.name: Diana Thurmann
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). On 1 May briefly active in #gdc-nonen-sync clarifying that Josh's Card Carousel announcement includes a Confluence docs link. Last DM with Mike was 22 April — receiving the Clarity API key and usage instructions (10 calls/day strict limit) for Viktor-based analytics automation. Previously created #gdc-nonen-market-updates channel (21 April) and announced Iceland geo launch in #core-division (16 April).

people.name: Colin Brannigan
people.summary: No activity on 3 May (Sunday). On 29 April active in DMs with Mike discussing the freebets.com Bet Finder product (noting UI is clunky but Alex's betslip element is being integrated), the product engineer candidate, and product roadmap alignment. On 27 April in #core-division-leads posted a Q1 earnings call prep request — asking division leads for wins across AI transformation, channel diversification, geo growth, SEO, and product campaigns. Also raised NZ affiliate marketing regulatory risk, flagging a strong likelihood that NZ will close off the affiliate channel entirely.
